items.title: RBI’s new model risk moment
items.text: The Reserve Bank of India's new paper expands model risk management beyond credit. It now encompasses broader governance across business and technology functions. Institutions must enhance accountability and implement enterprise-wide model oversight. AI-specific risks and third-party model responsibilities are also addressed. This move aligns India with global best practices in financial regulation.

items.title: IDC study highlights identity resilience gaps as AI adoption accelerates
items.text: Many organizations face identity management gaps for AI systems. Most businesses experienced cyber incidents, highlighting security vulnerabilities. Improved collaboration between IT and security teams is now essential. Organizations are also struggling with cyber resilience and recovery planning. A new assessment tool helps evaluate cyber readiness across key areas.

items.title: Why CISOs in India should rethink identity security in the age of artificial intelligence
items.text: Indian CISOs are prioritizing identity security as AI adoption accelerates across industries. Many organizations face delays in AI initiatives due to identity-related security friction. Reducing unnecessary access and replacing permanent privileges with temporary access are key actions. Credential security must be treated as a leadership priority, not just technical maintenance. Building resilience through stronger identity controls protects businesses from AI-driven exploitation.

items.title: TRAI likely to tweak regulation for spam data sharing by call management apps soon
items.text: TRAI plans to amend regulations for commercial communications soon. Call management apps may share user-reported spam details with telecom operators. This aims to strengthen the fight against unwanted calls and messages. The regulator is examining stakeholder feedback on the proposed changes.

items.title: AI is reshaping cybersecurity - for better and worse
items.text: Artificial intelligence is transforming cybersecurity for both defenders and attackers. Machine learning models improve detection and response capabilities significantly. However, threat actors also leverage AI for more sophisticated phishing campaigns. Organizations must now focus on responsible AI deployment and robust governance. This evolving landscape necessitates continuous adaptation and human oversight for cyber resilience.

items.title: India must build resilient software and hardware systems to withstand cyber threats
items.text: India must build strong and reliable software and hardware systems that can keep working even during cyberattacks. This resilience is important to support daily life and make it easier to do business as the country becomes more digital, said S. Krishnan, Secretary of the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY), on Monday.

items.title: India considers stronger safeguards as online fraud surges
items.text: India's central bank is seeking recommendations to tackle a surge in online fraud as scammers siphon off billions of dollars annually from users of its widely used digital payments network. Users can send the bank recommendations until May 9.

items.title: Why Zero Trust is becoming non-negotiable
items.text: Cloud transformation necessitates Zero Trust for modern enterprise security. Traditional perimeters are collapsing, making identity the new attack surface. AI adoption and multi-cloud complexity increase security challenges significantly. Zero Trust ensures continuous verification for all users and systems. This principle is vital for business resilience and responsible AI integration.

items.title: Beyond the blind trust: The hard questions security leaders must ask about AI
items.text: Enterprise technology faces risks with rapid AI adoption and slow integration. CISOs must enable safe AI implementation by addressing people, process, and technology. AI literacy and developer awareness are crucial for mitigating human-driven errors. Robust processes and least privilege principles build essential AI guardrails. Technology provides control and visibility, ensuring explainability and cost management.

items.title: Digital trust in 2026: Why privacy must be paired with accountability
items.text: Digital platforms grapple with balancing user privacy and preventing cybercrime. Concerns arise over platforms enabling child abuse and drug trafficking channels. Cybercriminals exploit communication tools for credential theft and illicit activity coordination. Platforms must develop safeguards against misuse while protecting valid privacy rights. Achieving this balance is crucial for trusted digital ecosystems moving forward.

items.title: New InfraTrust report reveals infrastructure flaws admins should patch first
items.text: Eclypsium has launched InfraTrust, a new infrastructure cybersecurity knowledge base and monthly InfraTrust Pulse report designed to help organizations prioritize vulnerabilities affecting infrastructure, firmware, networking, and edge devices. [...]

items.title: Adobe Chrome extension flaw let sites access private WhatsApp chats
items.text: The Adobe Acrobat extension for Chrome could be used to access conversations and data rendered in WhatsApp Web without any form of authentication. [...]

items.title: Hackers Exploit Windmill Flaw to Read Arbitrary Server Files Without Authentication
items.text: A high-severity security flaw impacting open-source developer platform Windmill has come under active exploitation in the wild, per VulnCheck. The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026-29059 (CVSS score: 7.5), a case of unauthenticated path traversal impacting Windmill's "get_log_file" endpoint ("/api/w/{workspace}/jobs_u/get_log_file/{filename}"). "The filename parameter is concatenated into

items.title: The Fastest Path to AI Adoption Runs Through Security
items.text: Security leaders who build fast, visible paths to AI adoption are becoming the most valued partners in their organizations. AI governance done right gives security teams the visibility they need, employees the tools they want, and CISOs the strategic influence they have earned. According to McKinsey's State of AI report, 76 percent of employees now use AI in some capacity at work, up from 55

items.title: CISA orders urgent action on actively exploited Langflow RCE flaw
items.text: The Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Tuesday ordered U.S. government agencies to prioritize patching an actively exploited vulnerability in the Langflow visual framework for building AI agents. [...]

items.title: OpenAI Says Its AI Models Escaped Sandbox, Targeted Hugging Face to Cheat Benchmark
items.text: OpenAI on Tuesday said a combination of its artificial intelligence (AI) models, including GPT-5.6 Sol and an "even more capable pre-release model," was behind the security incident that targeted Hugging Face's production infrastructure last week. The AI company said the models were operating with "reduced cyber refusals for evaluation purposes" that might otherwise limit their ability to

items.title: Why Modern SOCs Need Multi-Layered Detections
items.text: The cycle is over. For years, cybersecurity followed a familiar pattern: defenses improved, attackers adapted, and the back-and-forth continued. Today, AI-equipped attackers are simply outpacing defenses. Most intrusions now bypass endpoint and malware-based detection entirely. The CrowdStrike Global Threat Report estimates around 79% of attacks are malware-free, as threat actors rely on

items.title: Stop renting storage space — this lifetime 2TB plan is yours for $59
items.text: Cloud storage costs tend to creep up over time, since most services charge monthly or annually for as long as you use them. FileJump's Lifetime Plan skips that model entirely, offering 2TB of cloud storage for a single payment of $59 (MSRP $467). [...]

items.title: Microsoft to stop Exchange 2016 / 2019 security updates in October
items.text: Microsoft has reminded customers that it will stop shipping security updates for Exchange 2016 and 2019 through the Extended Security Update (ESU) program in October. [...]

items.title: Chick-fil-A discloses data breach after credential stuffing attacks
items.text: American fast food restaurant chain Chick-fil-A is notifying customers of a data breach after their accounts were hacked in a wave of recent credential stuffing attacks. [...]

items.title: Police Dismantle Kratos Phishing Kit Built to Steal Microsoft 365 Sessions and Bypass MFA
items.text: German and US law enforcement have taken down the core infrastructure of Kratos, described by German investigators as one of the world's most widely used criminal phishing kits, and Indonesian authorities arrested the man they say developed and ran it. In a joint announcement on Monday, the Frankfurt public prosecutor's cybercrime unit (ZIT) and Germany's Federal Criminal Police Office (BKA)

items.title: Trojanized Newtonsoft.Json Fork Hides Game-Rigging Code in a Working Library
items.text: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a NuGet typosquat that's unlike the typical information-stealing malware distributed via package registries: usual info-stealers: it's designed to rig live game results on Digitain. The package, named "Newtonsoftt.Json.Net," masquerades as the Newtonsoft.Json library and is a trojanized fork. Seven versions of the package have been published to the

items.title: OpenAI says its AI models hacked Hugging Face during testing
items.text: OpenAI says its AI models, including GPT‑5.6 Sol and a pre-release model, hacked into the Hugging Face artificial intelligence repository while being tested in a sandboxed testing environment. [...]

items.title: Microsoft Azure DevOps MCP Flaw Lets Hidden PR Comments Hijack AI Review Agents
items.text: A single invisible comment in an Azure DevOps pull request can turn a reviewer's own AI coding agent against them, driving it into projects the attacker has no rights to reach and quietly leaking what it finds. The flaw is in Microsoft's official Azure DevOps MCP server, and it works because one of its tools returns pull request descriptions without a prompt-injection guardrail the company had

items.title: Police dismantle Kratos phishing platform, arrest developer
items.text: Authorities in Germany and the U.S. dismantled the central infrastructure of Kratos, a phishing-as-a-service (PhaaS) platform with global reach, and its developer was arrested in Indonesia. [...]

items.title: FakeGit campaign uses 7,600 GitHub repos to push SmartLoader malware
items.text: A large-scale operation dubbed 'FakeGit' is pushing SmartLoader and StealC malware through 7,600 malicious GitHub repositories that accumulated more than 14 million downloads. [...]

items.title: Critical SharePoint RCE flaw exploited to steal machine keys
items.text: Hackers are actively exploiting the critical CVE-2026-50522 vulnerability in Microsoft SharePoint to steal machine keys and maintain access even after affected servers are patched. [...]

items.title: Apple Fixes Hide My Email Bug That Exposed Real Addresses in Mail Logs
items.text: Apple has moved to address a security flaw in its Hide My Email service that enabled users' real email addresses to be unmasked, effectively undermining the feature's privacy guarantees. 404 Media reported Tuesday that a fix for the issue was deployed by Apple on July 3, 2026, after more than a year, when it was disclosed to the company by Tyler Murphy, co-founder of EasyOptOuts. Hide My Email

items.title: AWS Kiro Flaw Let a Poisoned Web Page Rewrite Its Config and Run Code
items.text: Hidden text on a web page was enough to make Kiro, AWS's agentic coding IDE, rewrite its own configuration file and run an attacker's code on a developer's machine, with no approval step able to stop it. Intezer, in research with Kodem Security, found that a request as ordinary as asking Kiro to summarize a page could end in remote code execution. AWS has patched the issue, and no CVE has been

items.title: Google Launches Gemini 3.5 Flash Cyber AI to Find and Fix Software Vulnerabilities
items.text: Google's DeepMind on Tuesday announced the release of Gemini 3.5 Flash Cyber, a specialized artificial intelligence (AI) model built atop 3.5 Flash that's designed to discover, validate, and patch vulnerabilities quickly and efficiently. According to the tech giant, the model will be exclusively available to governments and trusted partners via CodeMender as part of a limited-access pilot

items.text: View CSAF Summary SIDIS Secured SmartPlug before V7.26.0310 is affected by multiple vulnerabilities in the components OpenSSL, OpenSSH, and several other packages as described below. Siemens has released a new version of SIDIS Secured SmartPlug and recommends to update to the latest version. The following versions of Siemens SIDIS Secured SmartPlug are affected: SIDIS Secured SmartPlug vers:intdot/<7.26.0310  CVSS Vendor Equipment Vulnerabilities v3 9.8 Siemens Siemens SIDIS Secured SmartPlug Improper Enforcement of Message Integrity During Transmission in a Communication Channel, Reus

items.title: CISA Adds Four Known Exploited Vulnerabilities to Catalog
items.text: CISA has added four new vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog , based on evidence of active exploitation. CVE-2021-27137 DD-WRT Stack-Based Buffer Overflow Vulnerability CVE-2026-0770 Langflow Inclusion of Functionality from Untrusted Control Sphere Vulnerability   CVE-2026-63030 WordPress Core Interpretation Conflict Vulnerability   CVE-2026-60137 WordPress Core SQL Injection Vulnerability These types of vulnerabilities are frequent attack vectors for malicious cyber actors and pose significant risks to the federal enterprise. Binding Operational Direc

items.title: Siemens IAM Client
items.text: View CSAF Summary Multiple Siemens products are affected by unquoted search path vulnerability in IAM Client. This could allow an authenticated local attacker to perform privilege escalation. Siemens has released new versions for several affected products and recommends to update to the latest versions. Siemens is preparing further fix versions and recommends countermeasures for products where fixes are not, or not yet available. The following versions of Siemens IAM Client are affected: COMOS V10.4.5 vers:intdot/<10.4.5.0.2  COMOS V10.6 vers:intdot/<10.6.1  Designcenter NX ver

items.title: Siemens RUGGEDCOM APE1808 with Palo Alto Networks Virtual NGFW
items.text: View CSAF Summary Palo Alto Networks has published [1] information on vulnerabilities in PAN-OS. This advisory lists the related Siemens Industrial products affected by these vulnerabilities. Customers are advised to consult and implement the workarounds provided in Palo Alto Networks' upstream security notifications. items.title: Siemens CADRA
items.text: View CSAF Summary CADRA is affected by multiple zlib and Foxit vulnerabilities. Siemens has released a new version for CADRA and recommends to update to the latest version. Siemens is preparing further fix versions and recommends specific countermeasures for products where fixes are not, or not yet available. The following versions of Siemens CADRA are affected: CADRA vers:intdot/<2511, vers:all/*  CVSS Vendor Equipment Vulnerabilities v3 9.8 Siemens Siemens CADRA Improper Input Validation, Incorrect Bitwise Shift of Integer, Out-of-bounds Write, Buffer Copy without Checking Size of In

items.title: Rockwell Automation FactoryTalk Services Platform
items.text: View CSAF Summary Successful exploitation of this vulnerability could allow an attacker to impersonate an authorized user on the FTSP server, resulting in unauthorized access to system configurations. The following versions of Rockwell Automation FactoryTalk Services Platform are affected: FactoryTalk Directory (FTSP) 6.60  CVSS Vendor Equipment Vulnerabilities v3 7.8 Rockwell Automation Rockwell Automation FactoryTalk Services Platform Weak Authentication Background Critical Infrastructure Sectors: Critical Manufacturing Countries/Areas Deployed: Worldwide Company Headquarters Location:

note: India-first CTI from free, purpose-built sources (ET CISO India, CISA KEV/advisories, security news RSS; optional OTX/ThreatFox with free keys). CERT-In has no working feed. No social-media scraping. Intel is enrichment — alerts are simulated and human-gated.
